metadata.data_source: Multiple sources including CBOE, FRED, Yahoo Finance, web research
metadata.disclaimer: This data is for informational and educational purposes only. Not financial advice.

vix.name: CBOE Volatility Index (VIX)
vix.interpretation: Low volatility - calm market conditions. VIX below long-term average suggests complacency.
vix.methodology_note: Based on S&P 500 index options prices

put_call_ratio.name: CBOE Put/Call Ratio
put_call_ratio.interpretation: Balanced to slightly bullish sentiment. Equity P/C at 0.55 shows bullishness, while index P/C at 1.18 shows hedging activity.
put_call_ratio.methodology_note: Daily put volume divided by call volume across all options

fear_greed_index.name: CNN Fear & Greed Index
fear_greed_index.interpretation: Greed territory - elevated risk appetite. Down from higher levels a month ago but still shows optimistic sentiment.
fear_greed_index.methodology_note: Composite of 7 indicators: momentum, strength, breadth, put/call, junk bond demand, volatility, safe haven demand
components: Stock Price Breadth (volume of advancing vs declining)
Safe Haven Demand (stocks vs bonds returns)

shiller_cape.name: Shiller PE Ratio (CAPE)
shiller_cape.interpretation: Extreme overvaluation. CAPE at 39.58 is 2.3x historical average and only exceeded by dot-com bubble. Implies low/negative 8-10 year returns (~2.3% annually).
shiller_cape.methodology_note: S&P 500 price divided by 10-year average of inflation-adjusted earnings

buffett_indicator.name: Buffett Indicator (Market Cap / GDP)
buffett_indicator.variant: Wilshire 5000 Total Market Cap to GDP
buffett_indicator.interpretation: Strongly overvalued at 219% - near all-time highs. Warren Buffett warned at 200% 'you are playing with fire'. Suggests significantly elevated crash risk.
buffett_indicator.methodology_note: Total US stock market capitalization divided by annualized GDP

yield_curve_10y2y.name: Treasury Yield Curve (10Y-2Y Spread)

inversion_check.note: Curve re-steepening after prior inversion is common before recession

yield_curve_10y2y.interpretation: Flat curve at 0.43% - below historical average. Signals slowing growth expectations. Not inverted but remains compressed.
yield_curve_10y2y.methodology_note: Daily difference between 10-year and 2-year Treasury yields
yield_curve_10y2y.recession_signal: Inversion (negative spread) has predicted every recession since 1955, typically 6-24 months in advance

sp500_technicals.name: S&P 500 Technical Indicators

price_vs_50dma.name: Price vs 50-Day Moving Average
price_vs_50dma.interpretation: 11.45% above 50-DMA - extended short-term. Suggests overbought condition.

price_vs_200dma.name: Price vs 200-Day Moving Average
price_vs_200dma.interpretation: 40.48% above 200-DMA - significantly extended long-term. Major deviation from trend.

rsi_14.name: 14-Day Relative Strength Index
rsi_14.interpretation: RSI at 70.92 - overbought territory. Potential for near-term pullback.

drawdown_52w.name: Drawdown from 52-Week High
drawdown_52w.interpretation: At 52-week high (0% drawdown) - strong momentum but no margin of safety.

drawdown_ath.name: Drawdown from All-Time High
drawdown_ath.interpretation: At all-time high - bullish but elevated crash risk from peak levels.

sp500_technicals.overall_interpretation: S&P 500 at all-time highs with extended technicals. RSI overbought, price far above moving averages. Suggests vulnerable to pullback.
sp500_technicals.methodology_note: Calculated from weekly price data over 5 years

sp500_above_50dma.name: S&P 500 Stocks Above 50-Day MA
sp500_above_50dma.interpretation: Healthy breadth at 56.77%. Majority of stocks participating in rally. Above 50 is constructive.
sp500_above_50dma.methodology_note: Percentage of S&P 500 constituents trading above their 50-day simple moving average

sp500_above_200dma.name: S&P 500 Stocks Above 200-Day MA
sp500_above_200dma.interpretation: Mixed long-term breadth at 56.37%. Slight majority above 200-DMA but not overwhelming. Some underlying weakness.
sp500_above_200dma.methodology_note: Percentage of S&P 500 constituents trading above their 200-day simple moving average

advance_decline_ratio.name: NYSE Advance/Decline Ratio
advance_decline_ratio.interpretation: Positive breadth with 1.32 ratio. More advancing than declining stocks - healthy market structure.
advance_decline_ratio.methodology_note: Number of advancing stocks divided by declining stocks on NYSE
advance_decline_ratio.note: Daily A/D ratio is volatile; A/D line (cumulative) provides better trend signal

overall_breadth_assessment.interpretation: Market breadth is generally healthy with 56-57% of S&P 500 stocks above key moving averages. Advance/decline ratio positive. Some divergence concern as S&P 500 is 40% above 200-DMA but only 56% of stocks are. Participation is adequate but not exceptional.

finra_margin_debt.name: FINRA Margin Debt
finra_margin_debt.interpretation: Margin debt near record highs at $1.22T. Debt-to-GDP at 4.1% vs 1.5% historical median signals extreme leverage. Creates fragility and forced liquidation risk in downturn. Historical precedent: rapid debt growth preceded 2000, 2007, 2021 crashes.
finra_margin_debt.methodology_note: Total margin debt reported by FINRA member firms, released monthly with lag
finra_margin_debt.regulatory_note: New FINRA intraday margin requirements effective June 4, 2026

near_term_stress.summary: Sentiment shows greed with low volatility. Some complacency but not extreme panic or euphoria.

valuation_vulnerability.summary: Extreme overvaluation by historical standards. Both CAPE (39.58) and Buffett Indicator (219%) near record highs. Poor long-term return outlook.

technical_regime.summary: Strong momentum but extended. Price significantly above long-term trend. Overbought conditions.

market_structure.summary: Breadth adequate but not exceptional. Margin debt at dangerous levels creates systemic fragility.

macro_environment.summary: Flat yield curve signals slowing growth. Not inverted currently but compressed.

key_warnings: CRITICAL: Valuation at extreme levels (CAPE 39.58, Buffett 219%) - historically associated with poor long-term returns and crashes
HIGH: Margin debt near record highs ($1.22T) at 4.1% of GDP creates systemic risk and forced liquidation potential
ELEVATED: S&P 500 40% above 200-day MA - major technical extension vulnerable to mean reversion
MODERATE: Sentiment in 'Greed' zone (Fear/Greed 59) with low VIX (16.70) suggests complacency
MODERATE: RSI at 70.92 (overbought) signals short-term pullback risk
mitigating_factors: Market breadth is healthy (56-57% above key MAs) - not narrow leadership
Put/Call ratio balanced at 0.85 - not extreme euphoria
VIX at 16.70 not showing panic but also not extreme complacency (above recent low of 13.38)
Yield curve positive (0.43%) - not inverted, recession not imminent
S&P 500 at all-time highs with positive momentum - trend remains up

base_case.description: Market continues higher driven by momentum, but with larger 5-10% corrections. Valuation expansion continues despite elevated levels.

correction_case.description: Market corrects on valuation concerns, margin unwind, or macro surprise. Not a crash but healthy mean reversion from extended levels.

crash_case.description: Major sell-off triggered by credit event, geopolitical shock, or forced deleveraging. High margin debt amplifies decline. Historical parallel to 2000 or 2007.

risk_management: Consider reducing equity exposure given extreme valuations
Monitor margin debt and breadth for deterioration
monitoring: Watch for VIX spike above 25-30 (stress signal)
Track margin debt monthly - rapid decline is crash trigger
Monitor breadth - divergence (S&P up, breadth down) is major warning
Track Fed policy and liquidity conditions

timeframe_considerations.short_term_1_3m: Moderate risk - overbought technicals, potential pullback
timeframe_considerations.medium_term_6_12m: Elevated risk - valuation stretched, margin debt high
timeframe_considerations.long_term_3_10y: High risk - CAPE suggests low/negative returns from current levels

overall_assessment.historical_context: Current conditions show similarities to previous market peaks: extreme valuations (like 1999-2000), record margin debt (like 2007, 2021), strong momentum masking underlying risks. However, crashes are hard to time - valuations can stay elevated for extended periods. The 'when' is uncertain, but the 'if' probability is elevated.
overall_assessment.disclaimer: This analysis is for informational and educational purposes only. It does not constitute financial advice. Past performance and historical patterns do not guarantee future results. Markets can remain irrational longer than investors can remain solvent. Consult a licensed financial advisor before making investment decisions.
